[TITLE]: Glastonbury's mass clean up begins as festival site trashed by days of partying - The Mirror
[METADATA/INTRO]: Glastonbury's mass clean up begins as festival site trashed by days of partying An estimated 2,000 tonnes of waste – that's almost 10kg per person at the 210,000 capacity event - is left every year after Glastonbury festival goers pack-up...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Glastonbury's mass clean up begins as festival site trashed by days of partying 
 An estimated 2,000 tonnes of waste – that's almost 10kg per person at the 210,000 capacity event - is left every year after Glastonbury festival goers pack-up their tents and head for home 
 It's the morning after the nights before at Glastonbury as the lengthy and costly clean-up operation begins. 
 Revellers begun leaving the Somerset site in the early hours having enjoyed music from stars including Elton John, the Arctic Monkeys and Lana Del Ray. 
 More than 210,000 pitched-up over the weekend and the days before - and despite pleas from organisers, they leave behind quite a mess.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Glastonbury 2017: Great clean-up under way - BBC News
[METADATA/INTRO]: Hours after the last chord sounded at Glastonbury the great clean-up has begun. The festival was brought to a close by Ed Sheeran on the Pyramid Stage, just six years after his debut at one of the festival's smallest venues....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Hours after the last chord sounded at Glastonbury the great clean-up has begun. 
 The festival was brought to a close by Ed Sheeran on the Pyramid Stage, just six years after his debut at one of the festival's smallest venues. 
 He attracted a much younger audience than Friday and Saturday's headliners, Radiohead and Foo Fighters. 
 There is no Glastonbury in 2018, meaning there are 731 days until Worthy Farm opens its gates again. 
 - Published25 June 2017 
 The BBC is not responsible for the content of external sites.
